name: Sinusoidal Position Encoding
oneliner: The original Transformer position encoding: sin/cos at geometrically spaced frequencies, added to token embeddings. Mostly replaced by RoPE.
explanation: From 'Attention Is All You Need' (2017). Solves the fundamental problem: self-attention is permutation-invariant, so positions must be injected. Each position gets a vector of sin/cos values at different frequencies, added to token embeddings. Design goals: unique per position, bounded magnitude, potential relative position awareness (PE(pos+k) = linear transform of PE(pos)). Wavelengths: 2$\pi$ to ~63K (geometric progression). Replaced because: (1) additive injection dilutes through layers, (2) model must learn to extract relative positions (RoPE guarantees it), (3) limited length generalization. Still used in diffusion model timestep embeddings.
fundamentals: PE(pos, 2i) = sin(pos/$10000^{2i/d}$). PE(pos, 2i+1) = cos(pos/$10000^{2i/d}$). Frequencies: $\omega_k = 1/10000^{2k/d}$, geometric from 1 to 1/10000. Added to token embedding: $x'(pos) = x(pos) + PE(pos)$. Properties: $\|PE(pos)\|^2 = d/2$ (bounded, constant). $PE(pos+k) = M_k \cdot PE(pos)$ where $M_k$ is a block-diagonal rotation matrix — relative position as linear transform. 0 learned params.
sources: Vaswani et al., 'Attention Is All You Need,' NeurIPS 2017, arXiv:1706.03762
